mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing units sold signals a strengthening demand for durable goods, which boosts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in industrial production signals a weakening supply chain, which compresses profit margins and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in weakening supply periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month rise in the unemployment rate above 6% signals a weakening labor market, which boosts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in weakening labor periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in the federal funds rate below 1.5% signals a monetary easing cycle, which compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP signals a strengthening demand for durable goods, which boosts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in industrial production and a 1-month decline in housing units sold signals a weakening supply chain and demand for durable goods, which compresses profit margins and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in weakening supply and demand periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month rise in the unemployment rate above 6% and a 2-month decline in payroll employment signals a weakening labor market, which boosts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in weakening labor periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in the federal funds rate below 1.5% and a 3-month increase in real GDP signals a monetary easing cycle and strengthening demand for durable goods, which compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing and strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
